title: Uragoga corymbifera
taxonomy: Uragoga corymbifera belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This species is native to tropical Africa. The genus Uragoga is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which comprises a large number of flowering plant species. Uragoga corymbifera is a shrub or small tree that is often found in woodland and grassland habitats. It is a member of the order Gentianales and is native to tropical Africa.
uses: Uragoga corymbifera has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine. It is reportedly used for various health issues, including fever and rheumatism. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its use in treating pain and inflammation. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. Traditional healers in West Africa continue to utilize Uragoga corymbifera in their practice, but its medicinal properties require further scientific study.

The plant's bark is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems.
